Original feedback: The checkout page keeps freezing when I try to apply a discount code on my iPhone 14. Happens every time. I've lost 3 orders this week because of it. Using Safari, latest iOS.

Classification:
CATEGORY: BUG_REPORT
SEVERITY: CRITICAL
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: frontend/checkout
PLATFORM: iOS
ENVIRONMENT: iPhone 14, Safari, latest iOS
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Go to checkout on iPhone 14 Safari, attempt to apply a discount code, page freezes. Happens every time.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Checkout freeze on iOS Safari when applying discount codes
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: CRITICAL

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: The checkout page freezes consistently when a discount code is applied on iPhone 14 using Safari (latest iOS). The user reports this is 100% reproducible and has caused 3 lost orders in one week, indicating the freeze prevents order completion entirely.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: frontend/checkout
- Platform: iOS (Safari)
- Environment: iPhone 14, latest iOS version

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
1. Open the checkout page on iPhone 14 Safari (latest iOS)
2. Add items to cart and proceed to checkout
3. Attempt to apply a discount code
4. Page freezes (user reports this happens every time)

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Reproduce on iPhone 14 Safari with a test discount code
- [ ] Check if the discount code input triggers a JS event that blocks the main thread
- [ ] Check if the issue occurs on other iOS devices or only iPhone 14
- [ ] Review recent changes to the checkout discount code component
- [ ] Check Safari developer tools for console errors during the freeze

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Exact iOS version (user said 'latest' but did not specify)
- Whether this affects all discount codes or specific types
- Network conditions during the freeze
- Whether the freeze is a full page hang or just the discount input area

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P1-CRITICAL
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Checkout is the revenue path. A 100% reproducible freeze on a major mobile platform directly blocks conversions. User has already lost 3 orders.

Original feedback: The API response times have degraded significantly since last month. Our p95 latency went from 200ms to 800ms. We're on the Enterprise plan and this is affecting our production systems. We've opened 3 tickets about this already. Account: api-team@bigcorp.com

Classification:
CATEGORY: PERFORMANCE_ISSUE
SEVERITY: CRITICAL
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: API
PLATFORM: API
ENVIRONMENT: Enterprise plan, production systems
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [API calls showing p95 at 800ms, up from 200ms since last month. Enterprise account.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: 4x API latency regression (p95: 200ms to 800ms) affecting Enterprise production
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: CRITICAL

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: Enterprise customer reports p95 API latency increased from 200ms to 800ms over the past month. This is affecting their production systems and they have escalated through 3 previous support tickets, indicating this is a sustained regression, not a transient spike.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: API
- Platform: API
- Environment: Enterprise plan, production workload

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
INSUFFICIENT DATA - steps below are what we know, not confirmed reproduction steps:
1. Make API calls to the platform (specific endpoints not identified by user)
2. Measure p95 latency
3. Observed: 800ms (previously 200ms)

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Pull API latency metrics for account api-team@bigcorp.com for the last 60 days
- [ ] Identify which endpoints show the most degradation
- [ ] Cross-reference with infrastructure changes, deployments, or scaling events from last month
- [ ] Check if other Enterprise accounts show similar latency increases
- [ ] Review the 3 previous support tickets for additional context

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Specific API endpoints affected
- Request payload sizes and query patterns
- Whether latency is consistent or shows spikes at certain times
- Traffic volume changes from the customer side
- Previous ticket IDs for cross-reference

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P1-CRITICAL
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: 4x latency regression on production API for an Enterprise customer. 3 escalations indicate growing frustration. API latency at 800ms p95 likely violates SLA thresholds.

Original feedback: The onboarding flow is confusing. I signed up yesterday and still can't figure out how to create my first project. The getting started guide links to a 404 page. I watched 3 YouTube tutorials and they all show a different UI than what I see. Free plan, Chrome on Windows.

Classification:
CATEGORY: USABILITY_COMPLAINT
SEVERITY: HIGH
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: frontend/onboarding
PLATFORM: web
ENVIRONMENT: Chrome on Windows, Free plan
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Sign up for Free plan, follow getting started guide, encounter 404.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Broken onboarding - 404 in getting started guide, outdated tutorial UI
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: HIGH

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: New Free plan user cannot complete onboarding. The official getting started guide contains a broken link (404), and external YouTube tutorials reference a UI that no longer matches the current product. The user has been stuck for 24+ hours unable to create their first project.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: frontend/onboarding
- Platform: web
- Environment: Chrome on Windows, Free plan

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
1. Sign up for a Free plan account
2. Navigate to the getting started guide
3. Click through the guide - at least one link returns a 404
4. Attempt to create first project - flow is unclear

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Crawl all links in the getting started guide for 404s and fix them
- [ ] Verify the onboarding flow matches current UI (check against what a new Free plan user actually sees)
- [ ] Audit official YouTube tutorials for UI accuracy and flag outdated ones
- [ ] Walk through the full new-user onboarding path on Chrome/Windows to identify friction points

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Exact URL of the 404 page
- Which specific step in the onboarding flow blocks project creation
- Chrome and Windows versions
- Current new-user activation rate (to gauge impact)

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P2-HIGH
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Onboarding is the activation gate. If the official guide has a 404, every new user who follows it hits a dead end. This directly impacts conversion from signup to active user.

Original feedback: Your billing page charged me $99 instead of the $49 I signed up for. Signup confirmation email March 1 shows $49/month Starter plan. Account ID: USR-44821.

Classification:
CATEGORY: BUG_REPORT
SEVERITY: HIGH
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: billing-service
PLATFORM: web
ENVIRONMENT: Starter plan, Account USR-44821
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Signed up March 1 for Starter at $49. Charged $99.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Billing discrepancy - user charged $99 instead of confirmed $49 Starter rate
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: HIGH

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: User signed up for Starter plan on March 1 with a confirmation email showing $49/month. Actual charge was $99. This is either a pricing table misconfiguration, a plan assignment bug, or a billing calculation error in the billing service.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: billing-service
- Platform: web
- Environment: Starter plan, Account USR-44821

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
INSUFFICIENT DATA - steps below are what we know:
1. User signed up March 1 for Starter plan
2. Confirmation email shows $49/month
3. Actual charge was $99

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Query billing database for account USR-44821 plan assignment and pricing
- [ ] Check if the plan_price field in the subscription record shows $49 or $99
- [ ] Verify the Starter plan pricing table configuration as of March 1
- [ ] Check if other March signups for Starter plan have the same discrepancy
- [ ] Review billing service logs for any pricing override events

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Invoice number and payment processor transaction ID
- Whether the database shows the correct plan but billing calculated wrong, or the plan was assigned incorrectly
- Whether this is isolated or systemic across March Starter signups

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P2-HIGH
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Billing bugs erode trust immediately. If this is a systemic pricing table issue, every Starter signup since the misconfiguration could be affected.

Original feedback: Can you add Salesforce integration? We need to sync customer records bi-directionally. Currently we export CSV manually every week, takes ops team 4 hours. Enterprise, 340 users. Would pay extra. Account: ops@megacorp.com

Classification:
CATEGORY: INTEGRATION_REQUEST
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: UNKNOWN
PLATFORM: UNKNOWN
ENVIRONMENT: Enterprise, 340 users
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Not a bug. Current workaround is weekly CSV export, 4 hours.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Salesforce bi-directional sync integration request (Enterprise, 340 users)
TICKET_TYPE: FEATURE
SEVERITY: MEDIUM

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: Enterprise customer (340 users) requests bi-directional Salesforce integration to replace a manual weekly CSV export that takes their ops team 4 hours. Customer has explicitly expressed willingness to pay extra.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: Data export/import pipeline (currently manual CSV)
- Platform: UNKNOWN (integration would likely be backend/API)
- Environment: Enterprise plan, 340 users

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
Not applicable - feature request, not a bug.

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Evaluate Salesforce REST API and Bulk API capabilities for the required data objects
- [ ] Assess whether existing data export infrastructure can be extended for bi-directional sync
- [ ] Estimate sync latency requirements (real-time vs batch)
- [ ] Check if other Enterprise accounts have requested similar integrations
- [ ] Scope MVP: which Salesforce objects and fields would be in v1

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Specific Salesforce objects and fields to sync
- Record volume per sync cycle
- Acceptable sync latency (real-time vs hourly vs daily)
- Data format and transformation requirements
- Authentication model (OAuth, service account)

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P3-MEDIUM
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Strong business signal (Enterprise customer, 340 users, willing to pay extra, quantified time savings of 4 hrs/week). But single data point - need to validate demand across other Enterprise accounts before committing engineering resources.

Original feedback: Cannot access account since password reset 2 hours ago. Login page accepts credentials but redirects back to login in a loop. Safari 17.3 macOS Sonoma. Also tried Chrome, same issue. Mobile app same loop. Account: j.walsh@designstudio.co, Business plan.

Classification:
CATEGORY: BUG_REPORT
SEVERITY: CRITICAL
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: auth-service
PLATFORM: web
ENVIRONMENT: Safari 17.3, macOS Sonoma, also Chrome, also mobile app
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Reset password, try to log in, redirect loop. Reproduced on Safari, Chrome, and mobile app.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Auth redirect loop after password reset - affects all browsers and mobile
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: CRITICAL

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: User is locked out after a password reset. Login accepts credentials but enters a redirect loop back to the login screen. The issue reproduces on Safari 17.3 (macOS Sonoma), Chrome, and the mobile app, which suggests this is a backend auth-service issue rather than a client-side problem. Started 2 hours after password reset.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: auth-service
- Platform: web + mobile (cross-platform, indicating server-side root cause)
- Environment: Safari 17.3 macOS Sonoma, Chrome, mobile app. Business plan. Account j.walsh@designstudio.co

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
1. Trigger password reset for account j.walsh@designstudio.co
2. Complete the password reset flow
3. Attempt to log in with new credentials
4. Login page accepts credentials but redirects back to the login screen in a loop
5. Issue persists across Safari, Chrome, and mobile app

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Check session token generation after password reset for this account
- [ ] Verify the password reset token invalidation is not interfering with new session creation
- [ ] Check for CSRF token cycling issues during the redirect
- [ ] Look at auth service logs for j.walsh@designstudio.co from the last 3 hours
- [ ] Test password reset flow on a staging account to see if reproducible

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Auth service logs for this specific account and time window
- Whether the session cookie is being set and then cleared in the redirect
- Whether this is specific to this account or a regression in the password reset flow
- Database state of the user's auth record post-reset

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P1-CRITICAL
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: User is completely locked out of a Business plan account. Cross-platform reproduction means this is likely a backend auth issue that could affect anyone who resets their password.

Original feedback: Login alert at 3:47 AM EST from IP 203.0.113.42 (Romania). I did NOT log in. Account lisa.t@company.com #AC-41209. Changed password, enabled 2FA. Need: Was data accessed? 30-day audit log? Other org accounts affected?

Classification:
CATEGORY: SECURITY_CONCERN
SEVERITY: CRITICAL
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: auth-service
PLATFORM: UNKNOWN
ENVIRONMENT: Account #AC-41209
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Security incident, not reproducible. Unauthorized login from Romania IP.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Unauthorized access incident - suspicious login from Romania IP on Enterprise account
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: CRITICAL

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: An unauthorized login was detected at 3:47 AM EST from IP 203.0.113.42 (geolocated to Romania) on account #AC-41209 (lisa.t@company.com). The user confirms they did not log in and has already changed their password and enabled 2FA. Engineering needs to determine if data was accessed, provide audit logs, and check for org-wide compromise.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: auth-service, audit logging
- Platform: UNKNOWN (login origin platform not identified)
- Environment: Account #AC-41209, organizational account

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
Not applicable - security incident, not a reproducible bug.

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Pull auth logs for account AC-41209 for the past 30 days
- [ ] Determine what actions (if any) were performed during the session from IP 203.0.113.42
- [ ] Check if the login was via password, SSO, API key, or session hijacking
- [ ] Run anomaly scan across all accounts in the same organization
- [ ] Verify IP 203.0.113.42 against known threat intelligence databases
- [ ] Confirm that the password change and 2FA activation successfully invalidated all previous sessions

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Session duration and actions taken during the unauthorized access
- Authentication method used (password, SSO, token)
- Whether other org accounts show anomalous login patterns
- IP reputation data for 203.0.113.42

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P1-CRITICAL
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Security incident with confirmed unauthorized access to an organizational account. Potential data breach. Must investigate scope and exposure immediately.

Original feedback: Everything is slow. Pages take forever to load. It wasn't like this before. Something changed.

Classification:
CATEGORY: PERFORMANCE_ISSUE
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: UNKNOWN
PLATFORM: UNKNOWN
ENVIRONMENT: NOT_PROVIDED
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [General slowness, regression from previous state.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Vague performance regression report - specifics needed
TICKET_TYPE: INVESTIGATION_NEEDED
SEVERITY: MEDIUM

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: User reports general slowness across the product and states it is a regression from previous behavior. However, no specific pages, actions, or technical details are provided. The mention of 'something changed' suggests correlation with a recent deployment or infrastructure change, but this cannot be confirmed without more data.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: UNKNOWN (user says 'everything')
- Platform: UNKNOWN
- Environment: NOT_PROVIDED

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Which pages specifically
- Browser and device
- Account ID for server-side metric correlation
- When exactly the slowness started
- Whether this is frontend rendering or backend response time

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P3-MEDIUM
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Performance regressions are important but this report lacks specifics. Worth cross-referencing with deployment history. If global metrics confirm a regression, escalate to P2.

Original feedback: Love the new calendar view! Makes sprint planning easier. Could you add drag and drop for moving tasks between days? Currently have to click into each task.

Classification:
CATEGORY: FEATURE_REQUEST
SEVERITY: LOW
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: frontend/dashboard
PLATFORM: web
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Not a bug. User wants DnD on calendar view.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Calendar view enhancement - add drag-and-drop task rescheduling
TICKET_TYPE: FEATURE
SEVERITY: LOW

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: Satisfied user of the recently shipped calendar view requests drag-and-drop functionality for moving tasks between days. Current UX requires clicking into each task to change the date, which creates friction during sprint planning.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: frontend/dashboard (calendar view component)
- Platform: web
- Environment: NOT_PROVIDED

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
Not applicable - feature request.

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Evaluate the calendar view component library for built-in DnD support
- [ ] Assess whether the current event handling architecture supports drag events
- [ ] Prototype DnD interaction and test with keyboard accessibility
- [ ] Estimate LOE (likely frontend-only change, API for date updates probably exists)

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- Calendar view component framework and whether it supports DnD natively
- Number of active calendar view users since launch
- Whether the task date update API already exists or needs to be built

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P3-MEDIUM
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Standard UX polish for a recently shipped feature. User is happy with the feature overall, and DnD would increase stickiness. Likely a contained frontend change.

Original feedback: I've been a customer for 2 years but considering switching to Competitor X. Pricing keeps going up, features haven't improved. Reporting is basic, no custom dashboards, mobile app crashes constantly. Renewal in June.

Classification:
CATEGORY: BUG_REPORT
SEVERITY: HIGH
AFFECTED_SYSTEM: mobile-app
PLATFORM: UNKNOWN
REPRODUCTION_HINTS: [Mobile app crashes described as constant but no specific trigger. Reporting and dashboards are feature gaps.]

=== ENGINEERING INSIGHT ===
TITLE: Mobile app stability issues contributing to churn risk (June renewal)
TICKET_TYPE: BUG
SEVERITY: HIGH

TECHNICAL SUMMARY: A 2-year customer reports the mobile app crashes constantly. This is one of three compounding issues (alongside basic reporting and no custom dashboards) driving them toward Competitor X. The mobile crashes are the only engineering-actionable bug here; the other two are feature gaps.

AFFECTED COMPONENTS:
- System: mobile-app
- Platform: UNKNOWN (iOS/Android not specified)
- Environment: NOT_PROVIDED

REPRODUCTION STEPS:
INSUFFICIENT DATA - user says crashes are constant but provides no specific trigger:
1. Open the mobile app
2. Use the app (specific actions unknown)
3. App crashes (frequency described as 'constantly')

INVESTIGATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Pull crash analytics from mobile app monitoring (Crashlytics/Sentry) for the last 90 days
- [ ] Identify top crash signatures and affected OS versions
- [ ] Check if crash rate has increased recently or has been consistently high
- [ ] If account can be identified, pull their specific crash logs

DATA GAPS FOR ENGINEERING:
- iOS or Android (or both)
- App version
- Specific actions that trigger crashes
- Device model
- Account ID for user-specific crash log correlation

PRIORITY RECOMMENDATION: P2-HIGH
PRIORITY JUSTIFICATION: Mobile stability is contributing to an explicit churn signal with a June deadline. Fixing crashes alone may not retain the customer (they also want custom dashboards), but persistent crashes make the churn decision easier for them.
